message.content:
You are confirmer #2, independent. Confirm a production-hardening fix is BOTH appropriate (correct, no regression, idiomatic) AND complete (fully closes it; for a code change the named regression test must DISCRIMINATE — fail if the fix were reverted). Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay
Fix G-002 in src/assay_engine/persistence/checkpoint.py
What: RESET lock_timeout on the bootstrap connection before it returns to the pool (session GUC leak).
Test(s): test_migration_lock_timeout_is_reset_on_the_bootstrap_connection
Steps: read the actual changed file(s); read the test and judge whether it discriminates; check no regression. For a doc/refactor item, judge correctness/coverage rather than revert-discrimination. Verdict CONFIRMED (both true) or CONCERN (something missing/wrong/under-tested). Be skeptical and specific.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""Durable run-state checkpointer — local, data-sovereign (ADR-0003).
2	
3	The orchestration graph persists its full state (current phase, gate decisions, locked
4	hypotheses, verdicts) through a checkpointer so a run can interrupt at a human-approval gate
5	and resume later. That state holds the entire analysis, so the store is **on-box only**
6	(ADR-0003); a SaaS checkpointer is disqualified.
7	
8	This module is the LangGraph checkpointer factory, ported faithfully from the prior
9	platform's hardened implementation and generalized (dataset-agnostic ``ASSAY_*`` env, no
10	config-file coupling — see ADR-0006). The hardened behaviors are deliberately preserved
11	because each prevents a real production failure:
12	
13	- a **self-healing ConnectionPool** (not a raw connection): when Postgres goes away — node
14	  sleep, restart, idle timeout, a network blip — a cached raw connection is permanently
15	  broken and every later checkpoint op raises, i.e. durability is lost exactly during the
16	  interrupt/resume the checkpointer exists to support. The pool runs a liveness check on
17	  checkout and replaces dead connections;
18	- a **session advisory lock** serializing first-time schema setup across *processes*, run on
19	  the *same* connection as the DDL (a pool would otherwise check out a different connection,
20	  leaving the lock ineffective);
21	- a **once-per-process-per-conn guard** (``setup()`` is idempotent but re-locking + replaying
22	  the migration query on every call is wasteful), with an in-process lock making the
23	  check-and-set atomic so concurrent threads don't both run setup;
24	- **credential redaction**: a backend exception (psycopg/psycopg_pool) can embed the full
25	  ``user:password`` URI/DSN in its own message, so the conn string is scrubbed precisely and
26	  the exception text via URI-userinfo + DSN-password backstops; the redacted message is then
27	  raised *outside* the exception handler, so the credential-bearing original is retained as
28	  neither ``__cause__`` nor ``__context__`` (a logger walking ``__context__`` cannot
29	  re-expose it);
30	- a single shared **atexit** pool-cleanup handler (one per process, not one per call) so the
31	  pool's worker thread is joined before interpreter finalization.
32	
33	LangGraph/psycopg are optional (the ``persistence`` extra) and imported lazily; the
34	credential-redaction and connection-resolution logic is pure and unit-tested offline.
35	"""
36	
37	from __future__ import annotations
38	
39	import atexit
40	import os
41	import re
42	import threading
43	from typing import Any, cast
44	from urllib.parse import urlsplit
45	
46	from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri
47	
48	_DEFAULT_PG = "postgresql://localhost:5432/assay"
49	
50	# Arbitrary fixed key for the Postgres session advisory lock that serializes checkpoint
51	# schema migrations across concurrent initializers. The value is irrelevant beyond being a
52	# stable per-logical-schema constant; "ASSY" as an int.
53	_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY = 0x4153_5359
54	
55	# Upper bound (milliseconds) on how long the cross-process advisory lock may be waited for
56	# during schema bootstrap. A stuck/crashed peer that still holds a live PG session on the lock
57	# must NOT be able to hang bootstrap forever (#129); lock_timeout aborts the wait. 0 disables
58	# the bound (block indefinitely, the pre-#129 behavior). Read fresh on each bootstrap (#F-033) so
59	# an env var set after import still takes effect; the module constant is the default + the value
60	# tests monkeypatch.
61	_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS = max(0, int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS", "30000")))
62	
63	
64	def _migration_lock_timeout_ms() -> int:
65	    """The advisory-lock wait bound, re-read from the env each call (#F-033).
66	
67	    Falls back to the import-time module constant when the env var is unset, so a test that
68	    monkeypatches ``_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS`` still drives the value.
69	    """
70	    raw = os.environ.get("ASSAY_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS")
71	    if raw is None:
72	        return _MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS
73	    return max(0, int(raw))
74	
75	
76	# Brief meta-lock guarding the process-local registries below (atexit registration, the open-pool
77	# list, the per-conn lock map, the pool cache). It is held ONLY for in-memory check-and-set — it
78	# is NEVER held across blocking cross-process I/O (pool.connection(), the advisory-lock wait, or
79	# setup() DDL). That blocking work is serialized per conn_str instead (#129), so a stuck advisory
80	# lock on one database cannot block initialization of an unrelated one.
81	_init_lock = threading.Lock()
82	
83	# Per-conn_str bootstrap locks: only same-conn_str initializers serialize; different databases
84	# bootstrap concurrently. Created lazily under _init_lock.
85	_CONN_INIT_LOCKS: dict[str, threading.Lock] = {}
86	
87	# Live pools cached by conn_str so the factory reuses one self-healing pool (and its worker
88	# thread) per database rather than opening a fresh pool on every call (#144).
89	_POOLS_BY_CONN: dict[str, Any] = {}
90	
91	# Pools opened by the factory, closed once at process exit via a single shared atexit handler
92	# (registering one handler per call would accumulate handlers across repeated factory calls).
93	_OPEN_POOLS: list[Any] = []
94	_atexit_registered = False
95	
96	# Strips ``://[REDACTED]@`` (URI userinfo) from any URI-like substring. '/' is allowed
97	# inside the userinfo so a password containing '/' is still redacted; only '@' (the userinfo
98	# terminator) and whitespace bound the match.
99	_CREDS_RE = re.compile(r"://[REDACTED]@\s]+@")
100	# Strips a libpq keyword/value DSN password token (``password=[REDACTED] / ``pgpassword=...``),
101	# handling quoted values that may contain spaces.
102	_DSN_PW_RE = re.compile(r"(?i)\b(password|pgpassword)\s*=\s*('[^']*'|\"[^\"]*\"|\S+)")
103	
104	
105	def _sanitize_conn_str(conn_str: str) -> str:
106	    """Return ``scheme://host[:port]/path`` with any ``user:password`` userinfo stripped.
107	
108	    Robust to malformed userinfo: a password containing ``/``/``#`` makes ``urlsplit``'s
109	    ``.port`` raise ``ValueError`` on access, so the whole parse (including attribute access)
110	    is guarded and falls back to the generic userinfo-strip regex / a constant placeholder.
111	    """
112	    try:
113	        u = urlsplit(conn_str)
114	        host = u.hostname or ""
115	        port = u.port
116	        scheme = u.scheme
117	        path = u.path
118	    except ValueError:
119	        return _CREDS_RE.sub("://", conn_str)
120	    if not host:
121	        return _CREDS_RE.sub("://", conn_str) if scheme else "<store>"
122	    if ":" in host:  # IPv6 literal — urlsplit drops the brackets; restore them
123	        host = f"[{host}]"
124	    netloc = f"{host}:{port}" if port else host
125	    return f"{scheme}://{netloc}{path}" if scheme else "<store>"
126	
127	
128	def redact_creds(text: str, conn_str: str = "") -> str:
129	    """Remove credentials a backend exception may embed in its message text.
130	
131	    Layers, in order of precision:
132	
133	    1. exact replacement of the known ``conn_str`` (handles any password characters, incl.
134	       ``/`` and spaces) — this is the precise layer and covers the real connection string;
135	    2. a generic URI-userinfo strip (``://[REDACTED]@`` → ``://``) as a backstop for any
136	       *other* URI in the text;
137	    3. a libpq DSN ``password=[REDACTED] strip for the keyword/value form.
138	
139	    Layers 2–3 are best-effort for *secondary* endpoints (e.g. a replica DSN) the caller did
140	    not pass as ``conn_str``; the primary connection string is always scrubbed precisely.
141	    """
142	    if conn_str:
143	        text = text.replace(conn_str, _sanitize_conn_str(conn_str))
144	    text = _CREDS_RE.sub("://", text)
145	    return _DSN_PW_RE.sub(r"\1=***", text)
146	
147	
148	def get_postgres_connection_string() -> str:
149	    """Resolve the Postgres connection string (``ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL`` env → loopback default).
150	
151	    The env path bypasses any external config validation, so loopback is enforced here at the
152	    point of use (ADR-0003 defense-in-depth): graph state must not leave the box.
153	    """
154	    url = os.environ.get("ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL") or _DEFAULT_PG
155	    return require_local_uri(url, what="checkpointer connection")
156	
157	
158	def _safe_close(pool: Any) -> None:
159	    """Close one pool, swallowing errors so a single failing ``close()`` does not abort
160	    cleanup of the remaining pools at interpreter exit (``close()`` calls a C ``finish()``
161	    that can raise)."""
162	    try:
163	        pool.close()
164	    except Exception:
165	        pass
166	
167	
168	def _close_all_pools() -> None:
169	    # Snapshot under the meta-lock before iterating (#F-020): _OPEN_POOLS is appended under
170	    # _init_lock elsewhere, and on a free-threaded (GIL-less) build a concurrent list.append
171	    # reallocation during iteration is undefined behavior. _safe_close never takes _init_lock, so
172	    # closing outside the lock cannot deadlock.
173	    with _init_lock:
174	        pools = list(_OPEN_POOLS)
175	    for p in pools:
176	        _safe_close(p)
177	
178	
179	def _register_pool_cleanup(pool: Any) -> None:
180	    global _atexit_registered
181	    with _init_lock:
182	        _OPEN_POOLS.append(pool)
183	        if not _atexit_registered:
184	            atexit.register(_close_all_pools)
185	            _atexit_registered = True
186	
187	
188	def _conn_init_lock(conn_str: str) -> threading.Lock:
189	    """Return the per-conn_str bootstrap lock, creating it under the brief meta-lock (#129)."""
190	    with _init_lock:
191	        lock = _CONN_INIT_LOCKS.get(conn_str)
192	        if lock is None:
193	            lock = _CONN_INIT_LOCKS[conn_str] = threading.Lock()
194	        return lock
195	
196	
197	def _cached_pool(conn_str: str) -> Any:
198	    with _init_lock:
199	        return _POOLS_BY_CONN.get(conn_str)
200	
201	
202	def _acquire_migration_lock(conn: Any) -> None:
203	    """Acquire the cross-process schema-migration advisory lock with a bounded wait (#129).
204	
205	    ``lock_timeout`` aborts the ``pg_advisory_lock`` wait if a peer holds it past the deadline,
206	    so a stuck/crashed initializer cannot hang bootstrap indefinitely; the resulting error
207	    propagates as a redacted ``RuntimeError`` like any other backend failure. set_config is used
208	    (not ``SET``) so the value can be safely parameterized.
209	    """
210	    timeout_ms = _migration_lock_timeout_ms()
211	    if timeout_ms:
212	        conn.execute("SELECT set_config('lock_timeout', %s, false)", (str(timeout_ms),))
213	    conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_lock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
214	
215	
216	def get_checkpointer(use_memory: bool = False) -> Any:
217	    """Return a configured LangGraph checkpointer.
218	
219	    ``use_memory=True`` returns an in-memory saver (tests / no-Postgres). Otherwise a
220	    Postgres-backed saver over a self-healing pool, with advisory-locked one-time schema
221	    setup. Raises ``RuntimeError`` (with credentials redacted) if the backend is unreachable
222	    or the extra is not installed.
223	    """
224	    try:
225	        if use_memory:
226	            from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import MemorySaver
227	
228	            return MemorySaver()
229	        from langgraph.checkpoint.postgres import PostgresSaver
230	        from psycopg.rows import dict_row
231	        from psycopg_pool import ConnectionPool
232	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
233	        raise RuntimeError(
234	            "Postgres checkpointer requires the 'persistence' extra "
235	            "(langgraph-checkpoint-postgres, psycopg[binary,pool]) — not installed"
236	        ) from exc
237	
238	    conn_str = get_postgres_connection_string()
239	
240	    # Fast path: reuse a live, self-healing pool for this conn_str rather than opening a new pool
241	    # (and its background worker thread) on every call (#144). The liveness check on checkout
242	    # replaces dead connections, so a cached pool stays valid across transient outages.
243	    cached = _cached_pool(conn_str)
244	    if cached is not None:
245	        return PostgresSaver(cast(Any, cached))
246	
247	    # Serialize bootstrap PER conn_str — NOT process-globally (#129). Unrelated databases
248	    # initialize concurrently, and a stuck advisory lock on one conn_str can never block init of
249	    # another. The brief meta-lock (_init_lock) is only taken inside the helpers, never held
250	    # across the blocking advisory-lock wait / DDL below.
251	    with _conn_init_lock(conn_str):
252	        cached = _cached_pool(conn_str)  # another thread may have built it while we waited
253	        if cached is not None:
254	            return PostgresSaver(cast(Any, cached))
255	
256	        failure: str | None = None
257	        pool: Any = None
258	        try:
259	            # Pool bounds are env-tunable (#F-040): the reasoning seam already runs 8 worker
260	            # threads, so a fixed max_size=8 can be saturated under concurrent reasoning+checkpoint
261	            # load on larger hardware. connect_timeout bounds an individual TCP connect so a
262	            # black-holed host fails fast instead of hanging bootstrap for the OS default.
263	            min_size = max(0, int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_POOL_MIN_SIZE", "1")))
264	            max_size = max(min_size or 1, int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_POOL_MAX_SIZE", "8")))
265	            connect_timeout = max(1, int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_POOL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT", "10")))
266	            pool = ConnectionPool(
267	                conn_str,
268	                min_size=min_size,
269	                max_size=max_size,
270	                kwargs={
271	                    "autocommit": True,
272	                    "prepare_threshold": 0,
273	                    "row_factory": dict_row,
274	                    "connect_timeout": connect_timeout,
275	                },
276	                check=ConnectionPool.check_connection,  # liveness check on checkout (self-healing)
277	                open=True,
278	            )
279	            _register_pool_cleanup(pool)
280	            # Bootstrap the schema once per process per conn_str. The advisory lock and the DDL
281	            # must run on the SAME connection (pg_advisory_lock is session-scoped); a pool-backed
282	            # setup() would lock one connection and DDL on another, leaving the lock ineffective.
283	            with pool.connection() as conn:
284	                _acquire_migration_lock(conn)  # bounded wait so a stuck peer can't hang us (#129)
285	                try:
286	                    # row_factory=dict_row makes this connection dict-rowed at runtime
287	                    # (what PostgresSaver requires); the cast asserts that runtime truth
288	                    # the type system can't infer from the pool kwargs.
289	                    PostgresSaver(cast(Any, conn)).setup()  # DDL on the locked connection
290	                finally:
291	                    # Best-effort unlock; the lock auto-releases when the connection
292	                    # returns to the pool, and raising here would mask a setup() error.
293	                    try:
294	                        conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_unlock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
295	                    except Exception:
296	                        pass
297	                    # Reset the session lock_timeout GUC (#G-002): the autocommit connection set it
298	                    # at SESSION scope for the bootstrap, then returns to the pool and is reused for
299	                    # all later checkpoint reads/writes — which would otherwise silently inherit a
300	                    # non-default lock_timeout the operator never configured for normal ops.
301	                    try:
302	                        conn.execute("RESET lock_timeout")
303	                    except Exception:
304	                        pass
305	            with _init_lock:
306	                _POOLS_BY_CONN[conn_str] = pool  # cache for reuse on later calls (#144)
307	            return PostgresSaver(cast(Any, pool))  # dict-rowed pool (row_factory=dict_row)
308	        except Exception as exc:
309	            # Build the redacted message here, but raise it OUTSIDE the handler (below) so the
310	            # original — credential-bearing — exception is retained as neither __cause__ NOR
311	            # __context__ (a logger walking __context__ would otherwise re-expose the password).
312	            failure = (
313	                f"Postgres checkpointer connection failed (is Postgres running on "
314	                f"{_sanitize_conn_str(conn_str)}?): {redact_creds(str(exc), conn_str)}. "
315	                "For tests without Postgres, use get_checkpointer(use_memory=True)."
316	            )
317	            # The pool may have opened before bootstrap failed — close it and drop it from the
318	            # cleanup registry so a failed init does not leak the connection/worker thread (#105).
319	            if pool is not None:
320	                _safe_close(pool)
321	                with _init_lock:
322	                    if pool in _OPEN_POOLS:
323	                        _OPEN_POOLS.remove(pool)
324	        raise RuntimeError(failure)
325	
326	
327	def configured_checkpointer(use_memory: bool | None = None) -> Any:
328	    """Return the checkpointer the environment selects.
329	
330	    ``use_memory`` (or ``ASSAY_CHECKPOINT_BACKEND=memory``) chooses the in-memory saver;
331	    otherwise Postgres.
332	    """
333	    if use_memory is None:
334	        use_memory = os.environ.get("ASSAY_CHECKPOINT_BACKEND", "postgres").lower() == "memory"
335	    return get_checkpointer(use_memory=use_memory)
336

content.text: The fix is at lines 297-304. Now let me read the test.

content.content:
560	    cp.get_checkpointer()  # distinct conn_str → a second pool
561	    # two pools opened and tracked, but only one shared atexit handler registered
562	    assert len(state["pools"]) == 2
563	    assert len(cp._OPEN_POOLS) == 2
564	    assert state["atexit_registered"] == 1
565	
566	
567	def test_concurrent_get_checkpointer_runs_setup_once(fake_pg):
568	    # #143: under real concurrent threads (not sequential calls), the once-per-conn guard must
569	    # still run setup() exactly once for a single conn_str. A trivially-fast fake setup() does not
570	    # exercise the race (each thread finishes the bootstrap section before the next is scheduled),
571	    # so we WIDEN the cache-empty→setup→cache-write window with a small sleep inside setup — this
572	    # makes the test discriminating: it fails ("setup ran 8 times") if the per-conn lock is removed.
573	    import sys
574	    import threading
575	    import time
576	
577	    cp, state = fake_pg
578	    Saver = sys.modules["langgraph.checkpoint.postgres"].PostgresSaver
579	    orig_setup = Saver.setup
580	
581	    def slow_setup(self):
582	        time.sleep(0.05)  # widen the window so concurrent callers genuinely overlap
583	        orig_setup(self)
584	
585	    Saver.setup = slow_setup
586	    try:
587	        barrier = threading.Barrier(8)
588	
589	        def worker():
590	            barrier.wait()  # maximize the race on the bootstrap section
591	            cp.get_checkpointer()
592	
593	        threads = [threading.Thread(target=worker) for _ in range(8)]
594	        for t in threads:
595	            t.start()
596	        for t in threads:
597	            t.join(5)
598	    finally:
599	        Saver.setup = orig_setup
600	    assert len(state["setup_on"]) == 1, "setup() ran more than once under concurrency (#143)"
601	    assert len(state["pools"]) == 1  # exactly one pool built despite 8 racing callers
602	
603	
604	def test_pool_closed_and_unregistered_when_bootstrap_fails(fake_pg):
605	    # #105: if schema bootstrap fails AFTER the pool opened, the pool must be closed and dropped
606	    # from the cleanup registry — not leaked.
607	    import sys
608	
609	    cp, state = fake_pg
610	    closed: list = []
611	    Pool = sys.modules["psycopg_pool"].ConnectionPool
612	    orig_close = Pool.close
613	    Pool.close = lambda self: (closed.append(self), orig_close(self))[1]  # record close
614	    Saver = sys.modules["langgraph.checkpoint.postgres"].PostgresSaver
615	    orig_setup = Saver.setup
616	
617	    def boom(self):
618	        raise RuntimeError("setup failed (schema DDL)")
619	
620	    Saver.setup = boom
621	    try:
622	        with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match="connection failed"):
623	            cp.get_checkpointer()
624	        assert len(state["pools"]) == 1  # a pool was opened
625	        assert closed and closed[0] is state["pools"][0]  # and it was closed
626	        assert cp._OPEN_POOLS == []  # and removed from the cleanup registry (no leak)
627	    finally:
628	        Pool.close = orig_close
629	        Saver.setup = orig_setup
630	
631	
632	def test_connection_failure_redacts_and_leaks_no_context(fake_pg, monkeypatch):
633	    # issue #P2: on failure the credential-bearing original must be retained as neither
634	    # __cause__ nor __context__, and the message must be redacted.
635	    import sys
636	
637	    cp, _ = fake_pg
638	    conn = "postgresql://[REDACTED]@127.0.0.1:5432/x"
639	    monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL", conn)
640	
641	    def boom(*a, **k):
642	        raise OSError(f"could not connect using {conn}")
643	
644	    monkeypatch.setattr(sys.modules["psycopg_pool"], "ConnectionPool", boom)
645	    with pytest.raises(RuntimeError) as ei:
646	        cp.get_checkpointer()
647	    err = ei.value
648	    assert "SUPERSECRET" not in str(err)
649	    assert err.__cause__ is None and err.__context__ is None  # no credential-bearing original
650	
651	
652	def test_versioner_publish_leaves_no_temp_files(tmp_path):
653	    # atomic publish must not leave .tmp- scratch behind, and an empty file is versionable
654	    store = tmp_path / "store"
655	    empty = tmp_path / "empty.bin"
656	    empty.write_bytes(b"")
657	    v = LocalDataVersioner(store_dir=str(store))
658	    digest = v.put(str(empty))
659	    assert v.path_for(digest).is_file()
660	    leftovers = [p.name for p in store.rglob(".tmp-*")]
661	    assert leftovers == []
662	
663	
664	# ---- pass-3: checkpoint pool/lock hardening ----
665	
666	
667	def test_initialized_conn_strs_dead_state_removed(fake_pg):
668	    # #F-025: the write-only _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS set was never read as a guard (the pool cache
669	    # is the real idempotency guard); it must be gone so it cannot mislead concurrency reasoning.
670	    cp, _ = fake_pg
671	    assert not hasattr(cp, "_INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS")
672	
673	
674	def test_migration_lock_timeout_is_read_fresh_from_env(fake_pg, monkeypatch):
675	    # #F-033: the bound must be re-read on each bootstrap so an env var set after import takes
676	    # effect, instead of being frozen at the import-time module value.
677	    cp, state = fake_pg
678	    monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS", "12345")
679	    cp.get_checkpointer()
680	    assert state["lock_timeout_on"] and int(state["lock_timeout_on"][0][0]) == 12345
681	
682	
683	def test_migration_lock_timeout_is_reset_on_the_bootstrap_connection(fake_pg):
684	    # #G-002: the session-scoped lock_timeout GUC set for bootstrap must be RESET before the
685	    # connection returns to the pool, so later checkpoint ops on that pooled connection don't
686	    # silently inherit a non-default lock_timeout the operator never configured.
687	    cp, state = fake_pg
688	    cp.get_checkpointer()
689	    assert state["lock_timeout_on"], "lock_timeout was set for bootstrap"
690	    assert state["lock_timeout_reset_on"], "lock_timeout was not RESET after bootstrap (#G-002)"
691	    # the reset ran on the SAME connection that set it (the bootstrap connection)
692	    assert state["lock_timeout_reset_on"] == state["lock_on"]
693	
694	
695	def test_migration_lock_timeout_zero_skips_set_config(fake_pg, monkeypatch):
696	    # #F-033: the 0 (disable-bound) branch must skip set_config('lock_timeout', ...) entirely and
697	    # still take the advisory lock — previously this branch was untested.
698	    cp, state = fake_pg
699	    monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS", "0")
700	    cp.get_checkpointer()
701	    assert state["lock_timeout_on"] == []  # no lock_timeout set
702	    assert state["lock_on"]  # but the advisory lock was still acquired
703	
704	
705	def test_pool_size_and_connect_timeout_are_env_tunable(fake_pg, monkeypatch):
706	    # #F-040: pool bounds + per-connect timeout must be tunable via env (no hardcoded 8 / no
707	    # unbounded connect against a black-holed host).
708	    cp, state = fake_pg
709	    monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_POOL_MIN_SIZE", "2")
710	    monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_POOL_MAX_SIZE", "32")
711	    monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_POOL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT", "7")
712	    cp.get_checkpointer()
713	    (kw,) = state["pool_kwargs"]
714	    assert kw["min_size"] == 2 and kw["max_size"] == 32
715	    assert kw["kwargs"]["connect_timeout"] == 7
716	
717	
718	def test_close_all_pools_snapshots_under_lock(fake_pg):
719	    # #F-020: _close_all_pools must iterate a SNAPSHOT taken under _init_lock. Discriminating: each
720	    # pool's close() REMOVES itself from _OPEN_POOLS (the same kind of mutation the real failure-
721	    # cleanup path performs). Iterating the live list while removing the current element makes a
722	    # plain `for p in _OPEN_POOLS` SKIP elements, so not every pool gets closed; iterating a
723	    # snapshot closes all of them. We assert every pool was closed exactly once — this FAILS if the
724	    # snapshot is removed (reverted to direct iteration), so the test genuinely guards the fix.
725	    cp, _ = fake_pg
726	    closed = []
727	
728	    class SelfRemovingPool:
729	        def close(self):
730	            closed.append(id(self))
731	            cp._OPEN_POOLS.remove(self)  # mutate the list mid-cleanup, like the real cleanup path
732	
733	    pools = [SelfRemovingPool() for _ in range(4)]
734	    cp._OPEN_POOLS[:] = list(pools)
735	    cp._close_all_pools()
736	    assert sorted(closed) == sorted(id(p) for p in pools)  # ALL closed, none skipped
737	    assert cp._OPEN_POOLS == []  # every pool removed itself
738	
739

content.text: Let me read the full FakeConn.execute to confirm the SQL-matching ordering is correct (RESET checked before set_config substring match).

content.content:
352	def fake_pg(monkeypatch):
353	    """Inject fake langgraph/psycopg modules so get_checkpointer runs the full hardened path
354	    offline, and reset the module's process-local bootstrap state."""
355	    import sys
356	    import types
357	
358	    from assay_engine.persistence import checkpoint as cp
359	
360	    state = {
361	        "setup_on": [],
362	        "lock_on": [],
363	        "unlock_on": [],
364	        "lock_timeout_on": [],
365	        "lock_timeout_reset_on": [],
366	        "pools": [],
367	        "pool_kwargs": [],
368	        "atexit_registered": 0,
369	    }
370	
371	    class FakeConn:
372	        def execute(self, sql, params=None):
373	            if "RESET lock_timeout" in sql:
374	                state["lock_timeout_reset_on"].append(id(self))  # #G-002 GUC reset
375	            elif "lock_timeout" in sql:
376	                state["lock_timeout_on"].append(params)
377	            # pg_advisory_unlock contains the substring "pg_advisory_lock", so test unlock FIRST
378	            # (#F-047): the explicit best-effort unlock in the finally was previously untested.
379	            if "pg_advisory_unlock" in sql:
380	                state["unlock_on"].append(id(self))
381	            elif "pg_advisory_lock" in sql:
382	                state["lock_on"].append(id(self))
383	
384	    class FakePool:
385	        check_connection = staticmethod(lambda *a, **k: None)
386	
387	        def __init__(self, conn_str, **kw):
388	            self.conn_str = conn_str
389	            self._conn = FakeConn()
390	            state["pools"].append(self)
391	            state["pool_kwargs"].append(kw)  # capture min/max_size + connection kwargs (#F-040)
392	
393	        def connection(self):
394	            conn = self._conn
395	
396	            class _Ctx:
397	                def __enter__(self_):
398	                    return conn
399	
400	                def __exit__(self_, *a):
401	                    return False
402	
403	            return _Ctx()
404	
405	        def close(self):
406	            pass
407	
408	    class FakeSaver:
409	        def __init__(self, arg):
410	            self.arg = arg
411	
412	        def setup(self):
413	            state["setup_on"].append(id(self.arg))
414	
415	    def _mod(name, **attrs):
416	        m = types.ModuleType(name)
417	        for k, v in attrs.items():
418	            setattr(m, k, v)
419	        monkeypatch.setitem(sys.modules, name, m)
420	        return m
421	
422	    _mod("langgraph")
423	    _mod("langgraph.checkpoint")
424	    _mod("langgraph.checkpoint.postgres", PostgresSaver=FakeSaver)
425	    _mod("langgraph.checkpoint.memory", MemorySaver=type("MemorySaver", (), {}))
426	    _mod("psycopg")
427	    _mod("psycopg.rows", dict_row=object())
428	    _mod("psycopg_pool", ConnectionPool=FakePool)
429	
430	    # reset process-local bootstrap bookkeeping
431	    monkeypatch.setattr(cp, "_OPEN_POOLS", [])
432	    monkeypatch.setattr(cp, "_atexit_registered", False)
433	    monkeypatch.setattr(cp, "_CONN_INIT_LOCKS", {})
434	    monkeypatch.setattr(cp, "_POOLS_BY_CONN", {})
435	    monkeypatch.setattr(
436	        cp,
437	        "atexit",
438	        types.SimpleNamespace(
439	            register=lambda fn: state.__setitem__(
440	                "atexit_registered", state["atexit_registered"] + 1
441	            )
442	        ),
443	    )
444	    monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL", "postgresql://localhost:5432/assay")
445	    return cp, state
446

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && git checkout src/assay_engine/persistence/checkpoint.py && git status --short && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_persistence.py -q 2>&1 | tail -12

content.text:
The file is restored (git checkout reverted my test edit). All tests pass.

Summary of findings:

**Fix (checkpoint.py lines 297-304):** After bootstrap, in the `finally` block, the autocommit connection runs `conn.execute("RESET lock_timeout")` wrapped in a best-effort try/except. This is correct and idiomatic:
- The connection is `autocommit=True`, so the `set_config('lock_timeout', ..., false)` at line 212 sets the GUC at SESSION scope (the `false`/is_local arg). `RESET lock_timeout` returns it to the server default before the connection goes back to the pool.
- It runs after the unlock and inside the same `with pool.connection()` block, on the same bootstrap connection.
- The `except Exception: pass` is appropriate — a failed RESET must not mask a `setup()` error, matching the established pattern used for the unlock immediately above.
- No regression: full suite passes 71/2-skip.

**Test discrimination:** `test_migration_lock_timeout_is_reset_on_the_bootstrap_connection` (tests/test_persistence.py:683):
- The `fake_pg` FakeConn checks `"RESET lock_timeout" in sql` FIRST (before the `elif "lock_timeout"` set-config branch), recording `id(self)` into `lock_timeout_reset_on`. Ordering is correct, so a RESET is not misclassified as a set.
- Asserts the GUC was set, that it was RESET, and that `lock_timeout_reset_on == lock_on` (reset ran on the same connection that took the advisory lock = the bootstrap connection).
- I verified discrimination empirically: commenting out the `conn.execute("RESET lock_timeout")` line makes the test FAIL with `AssertionError: lock_timeout was not RESET after bootstrap (#G-002)`. Restored, it passes.

Both appropriate and complete.

input.reasoning:
Fix at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/persistence/checkpoint.py lines 297-304 adds conn.execute("RESET lock_timeout") in the bootstrap finally block, returning the autocommit connection's SESSION-scoped lock_timeout GUC (set at line 212 via set_config(..., false)) to default before the connection returns to the pool. Correct and idiomatic: runs on the same bootstrap connection inside the `with pool.connection()` block, after the advisory unlock; wrapped in best-effort try/except matching the adjacent unlock pattern so it cannot mask a setup() error. No regression — full persistence suite passes (71 passed, 2 skipped).

Test test_migration_lock_timeout_is_reset_on_the_bootstrap_connection (tests/test_persistence.py:683) discriminates: the fake_pg FakeConn matches "RESET lock_timeout" before the set_config branch, recording the conn id in lock_timeout_reset_on; the test asserts the GUC was set, was reset, and that the reset ran on the same connection that took the lock (lock_timeout_reset_on == lock_on). Verified empirically by reverting the RESET line — the test FAILS with "lock_timeout was not RESET after bootstrap (#G-002)"; restored, it passes. Both appropriate and complete.

content.text: Confirmed G-002. Both appropriate and complete, with a discriminating regression test verified by revert.
